‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ

‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ

‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ E ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ
X X X X X

I want you to describe this grid to me, step by step. You don't need to describe the empty squares, which are denoted with "‚ĖĘ". Only describe the location of letters in the grid. Then you wait for me to say "What is your next command?", and then you continue with the next step. Take the size of the grid into consideration while giving commands. You can give only one command at each turn but it can refer to single or multiple cells, rows, columns or even the whole grid at once. When you have described everything, you say "Command: DONE". Only give the command and omit everything else.

For the filled grid above, here are the example steps.

Command: Put an E in second row third column

What is your next command?
Command: Fill the last row with X

What is your next command?
Command: DONE

Ok. Please do this for the following example, which is a 5 by 5 grid.
R R R R R
R ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ R
R ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ R
R ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ ‚ĖĘ R
R R R R R
What is your next command?<|end|><|start|>assistant

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to output the next command to start describing the grid. According to instructions, we give one command at a time, describing letter placement. We should not mention empty squares. We should take grid size into account. So we need to describe all the letters.

The grid example: 5x5. Letters: R in the first row all columns. R in rows 2-4 at columns 1 and 5. R in row 5 all columns.

We need to output next command after user prompts. So first command. As per example, we can e.g. "Command: Put an R in first row, first column". Then ask "What is your next command?". So we produce that.
